message:
fix(id-resolver): scope substring hash matches to the user-supplied prefix

`IdResolver` has two substring-match arms (one for the in-memory
`get_all_issues` path and one for the storage-backed
`get_all_issue_ids` path). Both extracted the hash portion from the
input via `split_prefix_remainder` and then handed that hash chunk
to `substring_match_fn`, but threw the prefix away.

That meant a query like `bd-7a3` would happily match `xy-7a3def...`
and `pq-aa7a3...` because the substring matcher only sees `7a3` and
doesn't know which prefix the user typed. In a project with multiple
prefixes that turned an "ambiguous match" into matches across the
wrong namespace.

Now both call sites keep the parsed prefix and, when present, retain
only candidates that start with `{prefix}-`. So `bd-7a3` stays
inside the `bd-` namespace, and the existing 0/1/many error/result
arms on `matches.len()` behave as before. Pure narrowing — if the
user typed no prefix (no dash in normalized input), the previous
behavior is unchanged.
